codex_response:
`98c475bf` is implemented end-to-end. The rule is: preserve the vertical side border, detect rows where columns `1` and `18` contain the same nonzero color as an isolated two-cell marker pair, and replace the interior with that color’s fixed template anchored on that row. The summary hint was used; it matched the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. No new reusable primitive was necessary.

Verification passed on both gates: `5/5` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. I also opened `[preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/98c475bf/previews/preview_01.png)` and the generated family looks on-distribution: one decoy template in the input, one or two side marker pairs, and the expected canonical output glyphs.
